page: The Vultr vcg-a40-6c-30g-12vram is a virtual machine within the Cloud GPU family. It features an Intel x86_64 architecture with 6 shared vCPUs mapped to 6 physical cores, operating at 1 thread per core. The system is equipped with 30.0 GB of system memory, providing a ratio of 5.0 GB per core.

For accelerated workloads, this server includes a fractional NVIDIA Ampere A40 GPU, representing a 0.25 slice of the physical graphics processor, which delivers 12 GB of dedicated VRAM. Local storage is provided via a 550 GB NVMe SSD. The instance also includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Utilizing a fractional GPU and shared CPU allocation offers a cost-effective entry point for workloads that require GPU acceleration but do not demand a full, dedicated graphics card. This resource density makes it suitable for smaller-scale machine learning inference, development environments, video encoding, and data processing tasks that benefit from CUDA acceleration without the expense of dedicated hardware.
description: The Vultr vcg-a40-6c-30g-12vram is a virtual machine in the Cloud GPU family, utilizing the Intel x86_64 architecture. It is configured with 6 shared vCPUs across 6 physical cores and 30.0 GB of system memory. For hardware acceleration, the instance features a fractional NVIDIA Ampere A40 GPU (0.25 slice) providing 12 GB of VRAM. Storage is supported by a 550 GB local NVMe SSD, and the network configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. By offering a fractional GPU and shared CPU resources, this server provides a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring GPU acceleration without the overhead of a dedicated card. It is suited for machine learning inference, CUDA-based development, and lightweight data processing.
og_description: The Vultr vcg-a40-6c-30g-12vram is a Cloud GPU virtual machine featuring 6 shared Intel vCPUs, 30 GB of RAM, a 550 GB NVMe SSD, and a fractional NVIDIA Ampere A40 GPU with 12 GB of VRAM.

tagline: A fractional GPU virtual machine offering balanced system memory and NVMe storage for cost-effective parallel computing workloads.
